evals.prompt: Use GitNexus to do a full bug hunt on my codebase. I want to find anything that could crash or freeze in production — concurrency issues, silent error swallowing, dangerous initialization patterns. The repo is indexed as bodyGuy.
evals.expected_output: Structured findings report covering all 4 hunt categories with specific symbols, severity levels, and fix recommendations.

assertions.text: Report contains severity labels (Critical/High/Medium) on findings
Report covers ≥2 of the 4 named hunt categories
Report names specific symbols or file paths

evals.prompt: My app sometimes freezes under load. Can you use GitNexus to trace where blocking calls are happening on the cooperative thread pool? Repo name is bodyGuy.
evals.expected_output: Hunt 2 executed with query→impact tool sequence, findings identify async→sync boundary violations with fixes.

assertions.text: Report investigates async/cooperative thread + blocking call patterns
Report shows upstream caller tracing (impact analysis)
At least one finding names a specific symbol with a fix

evals.prompt: Before we ship, do a security-focused architecture audit using GitNexus on the bodyGuy repo. I'm worried about path traversal, SQL injection, or any place where untrusted input reaches a dangerous operation.
evals.expected_output: Hunt 4 (input→sink) executed with Cypher queries, concrete security findings with file/symbol locations and severity.

assertions.text: Report investigates input→sink patterns (path traversal, SQL, injection)
At least one finding includes a specific file or symbol location
